questions.prompt: Why use `docker run --init` for many apps?

choices.text: Linux treats PID 1 specially: it must reap zombie children and signals require explicit handlers (default actions don't fire). Many apps weren't written as init programs — they leak zombies and ignore SIGTERM when running as PID 1. `--init` runs tini as PID 1; tini reaps zombies and forwards signals to your app, which now runs as a normal child. For Node/Python/Java/Ruby apps in production, this is recommended. In Kubernetes, the pause container handles PID 1 and you don't need `--init`.

questions.explanation: PID 1 is special in Linux. tini (via --init) handles the kernel's expectations so your app doesn't have to.

questions.prompt: A Docker host's disk fills up after a few weeks. Logs are configured to json-file. What's missing?

choices.text: Log rotation. The default json-file driver writes container logs to /var/lib/docker/containers/<id>/*-json.log forever, no rotation. Busy containers can produce GBs per day. Fix: set `--log-opt max-size=10m --log-opt max-file=3` per container, or configure defaults in the Docker daemon config (/etc/docker/daemon.json). Or switch to a shipping driver (fluentd, awslogs) that doesn't store locally.

questions.explanation: json-file driver never rotates by default. Set max-size/max-file or use a shipping driver. Standard production hygiene.
